leagueBriefing: Day 3 of the 2026 World Baseball Classic was defined by dramatic late-game heroics and dominant debuts. Ozzie Albies and Darell Hernaiz both delivered walk-off home runs for the Netherlands and Puerto Rico, respectively, while Team USA and Japan continued their perfect starts to the tournament with high-scoring victories. Italy also made a statement in their opener with a combined shutout of Brazil.

games.headline: Japan powers past Korea in Tokyo Dome slugfest to stay unbeaten
games.brief: In a back-and-forth rivalry showdown, Japan used a massive third-inning power surge to hold off a resilient South Korean squad. Shohei Ohtani and Masataka Yoshida anchored a lineup that traded blows with Korea all night, eventually securing the victory in front of a raucous home crowd to move to 2-0 in Pool Play.
bullets: Japan erupted for three home runs in the 3rd inning alone, courtesy of Shohei Ohtani, Seiya Suzuki, and Masataka Yoshida.
Seiya Suzuki finished the night with two home runs, leading the Samurai Japan offensive onslaught.
Japan's bullpen held firm in the final frames, with Taisei Ota earning the save to preserve the narrow lead.
The win marks Japan's second straight victory, putting them in the driver's seat for the Pool C top seed.
games.context: Japan remains unbeaten and well-positioned to advance to the quarterfinals as the top seed in Pool C.

games.headline: Soroka shines in return as Canada thumps Colombia in opener
games.brief: Michael Soroka made a triumphant return to the national team, pitching three effective innings and touching 97 mph to set the tone for Canada's dominant opening win. The Canadian offense provided relentless pressure, breaking the game open late to secure a comfortable victory in San Juan.
bullets: Owen Caissie delivered the early spark with a towering two-run home run in the third inning.
Michael Soroka allowed only one run over three innings, striking out three in his long-awaited WBC debut.
The Naylor brothers, Bo and Josh, combined for several key defensive plays and multiple RBIs to fuel the offense.
Canada’s lineup forced the issue late, scoring four runs in the eighth inning to put the game out of reach.
games.context: Canada opens Pool A with a statement win, boosting their chances in a competitive group featuring Puerto Rico and Panama.

games.headline: Albies makes history with first-ever WBC walk-off home run
games.brief: Ozzie Albies etched his name into the record books, blasting a three-run, walk-off home run in the bottom of the ninth to rescue the Netherlands from a late deficit. It was a historic moment for the Dutch, who appeared headed for an upset before Albies' heroics turned the game on its head.
bullets: Ozzie Albies' ninth-inning blast was the first walk-off home run in World Baseball Classic history.
Nicaragua took a 3-1 lead in the 8th inning behind a go-ahead two-run home run from Jeter Downs.
Lars Huijer earned the win in relief after the Dutch offense rallied with two outs in the final frame.
The victory is the 10th walk-off win in WBC history and a record fourth for the Netherlands program.
games.context: The Netherlands bounces back from an earlier loss to stay relevant in the Pool D standings.

games.headline: Azzurri arms and Nori's power lead Italy to dominant shutout
games.brief: Italy opened their WBC campaign with a defensive masterclass, shutting out Brazil behind a brilliant start from Samuel Aldegheri and a balanced power attack. Dante Nori provided the fireworks with two home runs as the Italians cruised to a lopsided victory in Houston.
bullets: Dante Nori finished 3-for-3 with two home runs and a double, driving in two runs and scoring twice.
Samuel Aldegheri struck out eight batters while allowing only one hit over 4.2 dominant innings.
Dominic Canzone broke the game open with a three-run home run in the seventh inning to make it 6-0.
Italian pitchers combined for 15 strikeouts while surrendering just four hits in the shutout performance.
games.context: Italy joins Team USA at the top of Pool B after a perfect start to their tournament run.

games.headline: Hernaiz's extra-inning blast keeps Puerto Rico perfect
games.brief: Puerto Rico survived a scare from Panama, winning in walk-off fashion thanks to a 10th-inning solo shot from Darell Hernaiz. In a classic pitcher's duel that turned into a late-inning grind, the hosts found just enough magic to stay unbeaten in front of an electric San Juan crowd.
bullets: Darell Hernaiz ended the game in the 10th inning with a towering home run to break a 3-3 tie.
Panama's Ariel Jurado was nearly untouchable early, tossing five scoreless innings with four strikeouts.
Puerto Rico rallied for two runs in the bottom of the 10th after Panama had taken a brief one-run lead.
The win improves Puerto Rico to 2-0, securing their spot at the top of the Pool A leaderboard.
games.context: Puerto Rico moves into a tie for first in Pool A, setting up a high-stakes clash with Canada.

games.headline: Venezuela pounces early to overwhelm Israel in Miami
games.brief: Venezuela showcased why they are tournament favorites, using an aggressive four-run first inning to bury Israel early. Luis Arraez and Eugenio Suarez led a relentless offensive attack that gave the Venezuelan pitching staff plenty of cushion to work with throughout the night.
bullets: Luis Arraez set the tone early with an RBI double in the first and finished with two home runs on the night.
Eugenio Suarez provided the first-inning exclamation point with a 386-foot two-run home run.
Venezuelan starter Enmanuel De Jesus retired the first 14 Israeli batters in a row to start the game.
Israel's Harrison Bader and Matt Mervis drove in late runs, but the game was already out of reach.
games.context: Venezuela moves to 2-0 in Pool D and remains a heavy favorite to win the group.

games.headline: Schwarber and Henderson lead Team USA to record-breaking win
games.brief: After a slow start, Team USA’s bats woke up in a five-run fifth inning to dismantle Great Britain in Houston. The American pitching staff was equally dominant, setting a new WBC record for strikeouts to keep the British offense completely grounded for the final eight innings.
bullets: Kyle Schwarber ignited the crowd with a 427-foot 'Schwarbomb' that gave the USA its first lead of the game.
The USA pitching staff recorded 17 strikeouts, a new single-game record for the United States in the WBC.
Gunnar Henderson was a force at the plate, going 4-for-5 with two RBIs and a double.
Great Britain's Nate Eaton hit a home run on the very first pitch of the game, which stood as their only highlight.
games.context: Team USA improves to 2-0, outscoring opponents 24-6 in their first two games of the tournament.

highlights.title: Team USA vs. Great Britain Highlights

games.headline: Taiwan stuns Korea in extra innings for historic first WBC win
games.brief: In a marathon battle at the Tokyo Dome, Chinese Taipei (Taiwan) overcame a late Korean surge to secure their first-ever WBC victory over their rivals. An extra-inning bunt strategy in the 10th proved to be the difference, breathing new life into Taiwan's hopes of advancing to the knockout round.
bullets: Taiwan utilized two consecutive bunts in the 10th inning to score the winning run, scored by captain Chen Chieh-hsien.
Yu Chang and Stuart Fairchild both homered for the second consecutive game to lead the offense.
Ace Gu Lin Ruei-yang delivered four scoreless innings to start the game, neutralizing the Korean bats early.
Closer Tseng Jyun-yue retired Korea in order in the bottom of the 10th to seal the historic victory.
games.context: Taiwan's victory keeps them in the hunt for a quarterfinal spot and marks their first win against South Korea in WBC history.
